title: 500 error when triggering webhook from Lovable app
symptom: When attempting to call the n8n webhook from the Lovable app, a 500 error occurs, and the workflow does not get triggered.
applies_when: Sending POST requests from Lovable app
not_this_if: Webhook is triggered successfully from other sources
Using GET method instead of POST
root_cause: The 500 error indicates a server-side issue, often caused by incorrect request methods or misconfigured webhook settings. In this case, the request method was not aligned with the webhook's expected configuration, leading to the error.
fix_steps: Ensure the webhook is set to accept POST requests
Check the webhook settings to allow multiple methods (GET, POST, OPTIONS)
Remove any unnecessary parameters from the webhook path
Test the webhook again from the Lovable app
agent_instruction: Configure the webhook to accept POST requests and remove any incorrect parameters from the path.
